reasoning: The response is strong overall and clearly structured around all required sections. For encryption, it chooses credible primitives (Double Ratchet + prekeys + sender-key style groups) and keeps decryption client-side, but the 1:1 handshake is underspecified versus a full proven X3DH-style flow (missing signed prekey/authentication details), and group forward secrecy is weaker than ideal because a shared group key model is simplified. Architecture is very well right-sized for a single VPS: monolithic Go API, Postgres, WebSocket, Caddy, local encrypted media, with a clear diagram and sensible justifications. Invite design covers generation, validation, one-time use, expiry, revocation, audit tree, and abuse controls; however, invite transfer resistance is mostly policy/rate-limit based rather than cryptographically bound to intended recipient. Security model is explicit and solid: it distinguishes content confidentiality from metadata exposure, covers malicious operator visibility, DB exfiltration, device compromise blast radius, and invite-system limits. Self-hosting guidance is practical (Compose, DNS/TLS/SMTP/storage dependencies, backups, restore, health checks), though operational guidance could go deeper on upgrade/runbook and secret rotation. Depth/precision is high with concrete schema and protocol flow, explicit trade-offs, and meaningful detail; minor precision gaps remain (e.g., per-recipient receipt modeling and some crypto protocol specifics).
